title: BiometricNet: Deep Learning based Biometric Identification using Wrist-Worn PPG

method.input_representation: raw PPG signal (band-pass filtered and normalized)
method.training_strategy: binary classification (one vs all) with weighted loss to handle class imbalance; five-fold cross-validation

evaluation_protocol: Five-fold cross-validation on a binary classification (one vs all) task for 12 subjects.

reported_limitations: Network must be trained for each new user
High enrollment/training cost for commercial deployment
Potential for performance shift based on changing cardiac or physical conditions

key_contribution: Proposed BiometricNET, a fully data-driven deep learning framework using CNN and LSTM for wrist-worn PPG identification
Eliminated the need for hand-crafted feature engineering in ambulatory PPG biometrics
main_strength: Effective at capturing temporal dependencies in cardiac signals using LSTM
main_weakness: Lack of scalability due to the requirement of a personalized model for every user
Evaluation based on classification accuracy rather than biometric authentication metrics (EER/FAR/FRR)
insights.notes_for_review: The paper reports high accuracy (up to 98%) but does not provide AUC, EER, FAR, or FRR, making it difficult to compare with other biometric systems.
